role: Per-unit gallery/decision mount that wires a shared fork-brain core into rendered units and batches route-back direction writes.
capabilities: Bind an inline 'Ask about this' brain affordance to a rendered gallery unit on gallery:rendered
Bind the same brain affordance to a decision card on decision:rendered
Build and toggle a brain panel with reply area, textarea input, and send button
Delegate brain turn Q&A to window.forkBrainCore.talk keyed by source/decision address
Collect gallery:direction events and flush them in a microtask batch via window.forkBrainCore.writeDirections
Expose window.forkBrainHooks.bindBrain for explicit projection binding
substance_why: It implements real event wiring, DOM construction, delegation to the core brain-turn engine, and includes a recent fix for decision-card binding plus microtask batching for route-back writes.
interactions_present: Click .brain-ask button toggles the brain panel open/closed
Click .brain-send button reads .brain-input and calls core.talk
gallery:direction events are captured and batched for route-back writes
interactions_missing: No Enter-key submit handler in the textarea
No explicit loading, error, or retry UI for brain turn or route-back writes
No close affordance for the panel except re-clicking the ask button
No cleanup or unbinding when a unit/decision card is removed from the DOM
No guard against rapid repeated panel creation or duplicate send listeners
No accessibility roles/labels beyond title and aria-live reply
No fallback if forkBrainCore is missing except a thrown error
performance_risks: Binds and builds DOM for every rendered unit immediately, regardless of viewport
Microtask batch of gallery:direction events is unbounded within a tick, risking large payloads
Repeated panel open/close recreates DOM and reattaches listeners for the same unit
No throttling or debouncing on high-frequency gallery:direction or rendered events
Window-level event listeners and per-element bindings are never removed, risking leaks on long-lived pages
reuse_why: The event contracts and per-unit brain-binding/routing-back responsibilities are well-defined and bug-hardened, but the DOM-construction and global-window wiring should be rebuilt in the new surface form.
decomposition_hints: Separate brain panel UI construction from binding orchestration
Split gallery:rendered binding from decision:rendered binding
Extract direction batching/flush into a dedicated route-back queue module
Move the core() dependency check into shared bootstrap/dependency injection
Isolate the explicit bindBrain API from the self-wiring window event listeners
